title: The Foundation of Modern Adventure Board Games (A comparative analysis / review)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YDlHWsYd23o

Daniel (@dungeondive) positions three classic adventure games—Talisman, Runebound, and Prophecy—as the foundational pillars upon which modern adventure gaming was built. Through a detailed comparative analysis across nine categories (base game quality, official expansions, house rule support, player agency, character progression, sense of adventure, and more), this video argues that these three deceptively simple systems represent the most elegant answer to scratching that tabletop RPG itch.

What makes this analysis compelling isn't declaring one winner, but rather recognizing that each game approaches adventure differently. Talisman offers unmatched character variety and nostalgic atmosphere; Runebound provides the most flexible sandbox for modular expansion; Prophecy delivers surprising player agency within elegant systems. The video celebrates simplicity as a feature rather than a bug—these games' lack of overhead is precisely what makes them ideal platforms for house rules and fan content. Any serious board gamer should understand why these three have maintained such devoted followings for decades.

----

Which of these three foundation games have shaped your approach to adventure gaming, and what mechanics from them do you find yourself missing in newer designs?
